 S5P MPC Product Readme Ozone Profile 02.05.00 
issue 2.3, 2023-05-26 Released 

S5P-MPC-KNMI-PRF-O3_PR 
Page 4 of 11 

1 

Summary 

This is the Product Readme File (PRF) of the Copernicus Sentinel 5 Precursor Tropospheric Monitoring 
Instrument (S5P/TROPOMI) Ozone (O3) Profile Level 2 data product and is applicable for the Near Real 
Time (NRTI), Offline (OFFL) and Reprocessed (RPRO) timeliness products. 

Product Identifier: L2__O3__PR 

Example filename: 

S5P_RPRO_L2__O3__PR_20180516T002049_20180516T020219_03045_03_020400_20230223T182808.nc 
S5P_OFFL_L2__O3__PR_20200715T095719_20200715T114047_14273_02_020300_20210909T004208.nc 
S5P_NRTI_L2__O3__PR_20200715T095719_20200715T114047_14273_02_020300_20210909T004208.nc 

The OFFL and RPRO data product has the following DOI: http://doi.org/10.5270/S5P-j7l9xvd  

The Readme file describes the current processing baseline, product and quality limitations, and product 
availability status. More information on this data product is available from the Sentinel product webpage:   

https://sentinels.copernicus.eu/web/sentinel/technical-guides/sentinel-5p/products-algorithms,  

and from the TROPOMI product webpage http://www.tropomi.eu/data-products.  

The data file contains the ozone_profile which gives the ozone concentration for 33 levels in the 
atmosphere, with a horizontal resolution of approximately 28x28km at nadir (28x35 km2 before 6 August 
2019). The estimated error originating from the measurement signal to noise is given in the data field 
ozone_profile_precision.  The  estimated  smoothing  error 
field 
ozone_profile_smoothing.  Advanced  users  are  encouraged 
the 
averaging_kernel in the group /PRODUCT/SUPPORT_DATA/DETAILED_RESULTS. In addition, the 
field  ozone_profile_subcolumns  provides  the  retrieved  ozone  profile  in  6  sub-columns,  with  an 
interval of 6 km from the surface to 24 km altitude and large intervals above. 

the  data 
to  make  use  of 

is  given 

in 

The qa_value provided in the data product is a convenient way to filter the data. Ground pixels for 

which the qa_value ≦ 0.5 should not be used, and ground pixels for which the qa_value ≦ 0.8 should 
be used with care as at least one warning occurred during the processing.  

Independent  preliminary  validation  by  S5p  Mission  Performance  Centre  (MPC)  Cal/Val  experts 
concludes that the NRTI / OFFL O3 Profile data is compliant with the requirements as defined in the S5P 
Calibration and Validation Plan [RD01], see Table 1.  

Up to date validation results are available in the Routine Operations Consolidated Validation Reports 
(ROCVR)  that  are  accessible  through  the  MPC  Validation  Data  Analysis  Facility  (VDAF)  website  at 
http://mpc-vdaf.tropomi.eu. The ROCVR reports are issued quarterly and reports released after March 
2022 include validation results of O3 Profile data.

3 

Product Quality 

3.1 

Recommendations for data usage  

For further details, data users are encouraged to read the Product User Manual (PUM) and Algorithm 
Theoretical  Basis  Document 
this  data  product,  available  on 
https://sentinels.copernicus.eu/web/sentinel/technical-guides/sentinel-5p/products-algorithms.  

(ATBD)  associated  with 

3.2 

Validation results 

Full and up to date validation results are available in the ROCVR reports that are accessible through the 
MPC VDAF website at http://mpc-vdaf.tropomi.eu. The ROCVR reports are issued quarterly and reports 
released after March 2022 include validation results covering O3 Profile products.  

4 

Data Quality Remarks 

4.1 

Known Data Quality Issues 

Currently, the following data quality issues are known, not covered by the quality flags, and should be 
kept in mind when using the O3 Profile product.  

Inspection of the data shows that for some of the retrievals unphysical solutions are found, which pass 
the qa_value filtering provided in Section 1. Therefore, it is recommended to disregard pixel that match 
the following criteria: 

Unphysical deviations from the a priori 

∑

𝑖=32
𝑖=0

⌈𝑛(𝑖) − 𝑛𝑎𝑝(𝑖)⌉ > 1.66 10−5

𝑚𝑜𝑙𝑒 𝑚−3, 

Where I is index for the altitude or pressure level, n(i) is the ozone concentration in mole m-3 and nap(i) 
is the a-priori ozone concentration. 

Data with poor fitting quality 

The  diagnostic  information  indicates  a  reduced  quality  of  the  fit  for  some  of  the  ground  pixels,  in 
particular  over  the  polar  regions.  These  data  can  be  filtered  by  removing  pixels  for  which  the 
cost_function data field exceeds 200. 

Radiometric correction: data with different soft-calibration file 

Data  between  25  July  2022  (orbit  24780)  and  15  January  2023  (orbit  27235)  use  a  different  soft-
calibration file for the radiometric correction implementation with respect to the RPRO dataset, which 
can affect the quality of the data.   

4.2 

Solved Data Quality Issues 

Solved with version 02.04.00 

The following data quality issues are resolved or changed with respect to the collection 2 (i.e., previous 
version 02.03.01) data: 

•  The Level 1B radiometric calibration of the radiance data has improved. For the Bands 1 and 2 
that are used for the ozone profile retrieval, there are still issues in the spectral region below 
approximately 310 nm. These are addressed with an updated soft calibration file. The method 
to derive the soft calibration is unchanged and described in the ATBD [RD02]. 

•  New surface reflectance information is used. A clear example of the impact is over the Southern 
Ocean near Antarctica. In the previous version the combination of auxiliary cloud fraction and 
surface  albedo  would  lead  to  non-convergences.  This  should  now  be  resolved  with  version 
2.4.0. 

 
 
 S5P MPC Product Readme Ozone Profile 02.05.00 
issue 2.3, 2023-05-26 Released 

S5P-MPC-KNMI-PRF-O3_PR 
Page 7 of 11 

•  High SO2 as determined in the spectral fit are now included in the qa_value. 

•  The maximum number of iterations is reduced from 12 to 8. This should have little impact  on 

data quality, because almost all retrievals that converge do this well within 8 iterations. 

Data gaps in NRTI data (solved with version 02.05.00) 

To meet the timeliness requirements, there is limited time available to  process the data for the NRT 
data. If the maximum time is reached, processing is stopped and remaining retrievals are not performed, 
resulting in data gaps between consecutive files. This only applied to the NRTI data; in the OFFL data 
stream all ground pixels were processed. 

4.3 

Data Features 

This section describes some characteristics of the data that might seem anomalous; however they are 
physically correct and not related to any problem. 

Pixel geolocation around North Pole (feature) 

The solar irradiance is measured on a daily basis over the North Pole at a reference azimuth angle to 
remove seasonal effects on the measurements. To this end, a yaw manoeuvre is executed when the 
instrument is still in radiance mode, causing possible distortion on the scanlines observed during this 
manoeuvre (i.e., crossing scanlines, “bow-tie” ground pixel shape instead of rectangular). This occurs 
at most during the last 26 seconds of radiance measurements every 15th orbit (once every 25 hours). 
Though this may seem anomalous, it is physically correct, and not related to any problem on the data 
geolocation. 

4.4 

Mission Operations Changes 

A change in the Copernicus Sentinel 5P operations scenario, increasing the spatial resolution from 7.0 
km to 5.5 km along track for all measurements, became operational starting from 6 August 2019, orbit 
9388. 

5 

Algorithm Change Record 

For a detailed description of the L2__O3__PR algorithms, please refer to the ATBD [RD02]. 

Version 2.4.0: Included SO2 column in the calculation of the qa_value. 

Version 2.5.0: Implementation of the “checkerboard pattern” (i.e., processing of half of the ground pixels) 
in the NRTI processing chain, to significantly reduce the processing time. 

6 

Data Format 

The product is stored as NetCDF4 file. The NetCDF4 file contains both the data and the metadata for 
the product. 

For OFFL data the product is stored as a single file per satellite orbit, for NRTI data the product is stored 
as multiple files (5 minute granules) per orbit. 

Please note that consecutive data granules of the NRTI product show an overlap of about 12 scan lines.  

Details of the data format are provided in the Product User Manual (PUM) [RD03]. 

6.1 

Data format changes

Version 2.5.0

the attribute ‘multiplication_factor_to_convert_to_DU’ has been added

6.1.3 

Version 2.3.1 

Version of first public release, therefore there were no Data format changes to report. 

7 

Product Availability 

The data are available from the Copernicus Open Data Hub https://scihub.copernicus.eu.  

The list of major mission data gaps due to acquisition faults or satellite/instrument disruption is available 
at https://sentinel.esa.int/web/sentinel/missions/sentinel-5p/mission-status. For those periods the data 
are permanently lost.
